newsletters.forms module

class newsletters.forms.NewsletterForm(data=None, files=None, auto_id='id_%s', prefix=None, initial=None, error_class=<class 'django.forms.utils.ErrorList'>, label_suffix=None, empty_permitted=False, instance=None, use_required_attribute=None, renderer=None)[source]

Bases: ModelForm

Form for creating or updating a Newsletter instance.

This form exposes the following fields for user input:

  • title: The newsletter’s title.

  • description: A brief description or summary of the newsletter.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ['title', 'description']
model

alias of Newsletter

base_fields = {'description': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'title': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}
declared_fields = {}
property media

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

newsletters.models module

class newsletters.models.Newsletter(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Represents a newsletter containing a collection of articles.

A Newsletter is authored by a journalist or editor and can be associated with multiple articles.

Relationships:

  • author: The user who created the newsletter.

  • articles: Articles included in this newsletter.

Notes

  • created_at automatically records when the newsletter was created.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

articles

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

author

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

author_id
created_at

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

description

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_next_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)
get_previous_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)
id

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>
title

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

newsletters.tests module

class newsletters.tests.NewsletterTests(methodName='runTest')[source]

Bases: TestCase

Tests for Newsletter functionality.

Covers:

  • Newsletter creation by journalists.

  • Only the newsletter creator can add or remove articles.

setUp() → None[source]

Create test data:

  • One journalist user (newsletter creator)

  • One other journalist user

  • Two articles

test_articles_can_be_added_to_newsletters() → None[source]

Test that articles can be added to a newsletter successfully.

This test directly adds an article to the newsletter’s ManyToMany articles field and verifies that the article appears in the queryset.

Steps:

  1. Create a newsletter authored by a journalist.

  2. Add an article to the newsletter.

3. Assert that the article is now part of the newsletter’s articles.

Returns:

None

test_journalist_can_create_newsletter() → None[source]

Test that a journalist can successfully create anewsletter via POST request.

Steps:

  1. Log in as the creator journalist.

  2. Submit POST request with newsletter title and description.

  3. Verify that newsletter count increases.

  4. Verify that newsletter author is set correctly.

  5. Verify redirect after creation.

test_reader_cannot_create_newsletter() → None[source]

Test that a reader cannot create anewsletter via POST request.

Steps:

  1. Log in as the creator reader.

  2. Submit POST request with newsletter title and description.

  3. Verify that newsletter count increases.

  4. Verify that newsletter author is set correctly.

  5. Verify redirect after creation.

newsletters.urls module

newsletters.views module

newsletters.views.add_article(request, npk, apk)[source]

Add an article to a newsletter.

Access Control:

  • User must be authenticated.

  • User must be a Journalist or Editor.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

  • npk (int) – Primary key of the newsletter.

  • apk (int) – Primary key of the article to add.

Returns:

Redirect to the newsletter detail page.

Return type:

HttpResponse

newsletters.views.create_newsletter(request)[source]

Create a new newsletter.

Access Control:

  • User must be authenticated.

  • User must be a Journalist or Editor (enforced by decorator).

Handles POST requests for form submission and GET requests to display an empty form. Sets the current user as the author on save.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

Returns:

Rendered form template on GET or redirect

after successful creation. :rtype: HttpResponse

newsletters.views.delete_newsletter(request, pk)[source]

Delete a newsletter.

Access Control:

  • User must be authenticated.

  • User must be a Journalist or Editor.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

  • pk (int) – Primary key of the newsletter to delete.

Returns:

Redirect to the newsletters list page.

Return type:

HttpResponse

newsletters.views.edit_newsletter(request, pk)[source]

Edit an existing newsletter.

Access Control:

  • User must be authenticated.

  • User must be a Journalist or Editor.

Handles POST requests to update the newsletter and GET requests to display the form pre-filled with existing data.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

  • pk (int) – Primary key of the newsletter to edit.

Returns:

Rendered edit form template on GET or redirect to

view on POST. :rtype: HttpResponse

newsletters.views.newsletters(request)[source]

Display a list of all newsletters.

Access Control:

  • User must be authenticated.

Retrieves all Newsletter instances from the database and renders them in the newsletters template.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

Returns:

Rendered template with all newsletters.

Return type:

HttpResponse

newsletters.views.remove_article(request, npk, apk)[source]

Remove an article from a newsletter.

Access Control:

  • User must be authenticated.

  • User must be a Journalist or Editor.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

  • npk (int) – Primary key of the newsletter.

  • apk (int) – Primary key of the article to remove.

Returns:

Redirect to the newsletter detail page.

Return type:

HttpResponse

newsletters.views.view_newsletter(request, pk)[source]

View a single newsletter and its associated articles.

Access Control:

  • User must be authenticated.

Also provides a list of articles not yet added to the newsletter.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

  • pk (int) – Primary key of the newsletter to view.

Returns:

Rendered newsletter detail template.

Return type:

HttpResponse
